pundit.name: Landon Donovan
pundit.take:
**I think if you're Gunner, you can't really be proud of leading, can you? It feels more like you're benefiting from the chaos swirling around you than any real strategy.** Look, I get why fans of Bubba G might think he's got the magic touch with France cruising to inevitable dominance, but let’s face it, playing DR Congo against Uzbekistan is like betting your house on a coin flip and calling it genius. But I guess when you’re at the top, even stumbling around starts to look like choreography. 

Now, speaking of choreography you just can’t look away from, Ghost Pepper Devin might want to rethink his strategy. I feel for him, I really do, but picking Haiti in the fourth round was always going to be an uphill climb. Spain's boundless potential can only cover so many draft sins, and the USA's inconsistency isn't exactly the cavalry he hoped for. As for Gayden, a.k.a. The Backpass Assassin, his mix of England's clear win over Panama and Jordan's spirited but futile performance against Argentina feels like a metaphor for his managerial skills: dazzling and hopelessly naive. Meanwhile, Mustard Boy Zach got a raw deal with Ghana's narrow loss to Croatia, but that one-point draw from Austria still leaves him lingering in fourth, bless his heart.

And what can we even say about The Noisemaker, Rafe? Losing to Gunner with Uzbekistan might sound unfortunate, but when your first three picks don't even qualify, it's like watching someone trip over their own shoelaces and then blaming the universe. Of course, Zach’s Brazil might turn things around for him with some magic of their own. But if I were a betting man, which I'm not, I'd say this whole competition is far from decided. Yet as sure as night follows day, Bubba G will find a way to stumble into more points, Devin will keep drafting disaster, and Gayden... well, aimlessly dribbling around the midfield is called passing for a reason.

pundit_takes.headline: Gunner's DR Congo Triumph Explains First Place Standing
pundit_takes.subtitle: Critics suggest same strategy could work wonders on actual soccer teams.

pundit_takes.headline: Gayden Boosts Points Despite Jordan's Predictable Defeat
pundit_takes.subtitle: Leapfrogs Zach by riding coattails of England's win over Panama.

pundit_takes.headline: Zach's Pointless Ghana Performance Raises Roster Concerns
pundit_takes.subtitle: Hopes for a comeback rest on the shoulders of Saudi Arabia.

pundit_takes.headline: Rafe's Mismanaged Uzbekistan Team Suffers Predictable Defeat
pundit_takes.subtitle: Demonstrates impressive commitment to not knowing anything about soccer.

rome_analytics_quotes: Rafe's three-match losing streak proves sometimes chaos is just plain losing.
Rafe drafted ghost teams, yet somehow isn't dead last. How is that possible?
Gunner absolutely sonning Rafe with a monstrous +6 point differential head-to-head.
Sim predicted Rafe's demise; who knew chaos had its limitations?
